conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can assess your situation accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the rules and I'm ready to help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old.

Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. You mentioned you're living abroad at the moment.

Can you tell me which country you're currently living in?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. And can you tell me about your employment status or situation in Spain? For example, are you:
- Working there
- A Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad)
- Retired
- Or something else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm just living there - not working for the UK government or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. A few more questions to assess your situation properly:

Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And does Alex live with you in Spain, or does he live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me here in Spain.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me assess your eligibility based on what you've told me.

Based on the official UK government rules I've reviewed, I need to inform you of the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Result:**

I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason:** While Alex is 13 years old and you are responsible for him (which meets the age requirement), the key issue is that you need to live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. You're currently living in Spain with Alex.

There are some exceptions for people living abroad - for example, if you're a Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad) or if you move to certain qualifying countries - but as a UK citizen simply residing in Spain without being a Crown servant, you don't meet the UK residency requirement.

If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK with Alex, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education/training) AND you live in the UK. Alex is 13 years old and you are responsible for him, which meets the age requirement. However, you are currently living in Spain with Alex, not in the UK. While there are exceptions for people living abroad - such as if you move to certain countries or if you're a Crown servant - you've indicated you're simply living in Spain and are not working for the UK government. The eligibility rules state you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant, but as a UK citizen simply residing in Spain without Crown servant status, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Spain with their 13-year-old son Alex. While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement as they are not living in the UK and do not fall under the exceptions for living abroad (such as being a Crown servant or moving to certain qualifying countries).

The eligibility rules state you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant, but as a UK citizen simply residing in Spain without Crown servant status, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement as they are not living in the UK and do not fall under the exceptions for living abroad (such as being a Crown servant or moving to certain qualifying countries).
